raw_title: Journalistic Roles in Light of Populism: How German Public Service Media Representatives Discuss Their Coverage of Populists and How They Perceive Their Role
raw_abstract: Public service media (PSM) face a democratic dilemma when covering right-wing populism: They are expected to be impartial and ensure pluralism in their reporting. This requires them to give visibility to a range of views, including those of far-right parties, while safeguarding democratic principles such as non-discrimination and human dignity. In this context, it is essential to understand how PSM representatives reflect on their reporting of right-wing populism. Drawing on the concept of journalistic roles, this study examines PSM representatives’ narrated role performance and role orientation in reporting on populism. Based on semi-structured interviews with twenty-six German PSM representatives, the findings reveal that journalists perceive past reporting mistakes and partly believe that PSM have contributed to the rise of populism. Regarding their role orientation, PSM representatives emphasize that they do not wish to exclude populist actors from coverage unless they make anti-constitutional statements, a stance they justify by the democratic election of these figures. More broadly, PSM representatives take on three distinct role orientations when reporting on populism: as disseminators, they aim to provide neutral coverage of events; as watchdogs, they seek to critically scrutinize populist actors; and as educators, they strive to inform the public about problematic developments within populist parties. Against the backdrop of the growing support for the German populist party Alternative für Deutschland (AfD), this study contributes to existing research by proposing a conceptual framework for reporting on populism and offering qualitative insights into how PSM representatives navigate their role in covering the AfD.
title_en: Journalistic Roles in Light of Populism: How German Public Service Media Representatives Discuss Their Coverage of Populists and How They Perceive Their Role
title_zh: 民粹主义下的新闻角色：德国公共服务媒体代表如何讨论其对民粹主义者的报道及角色认知
abstract_en: Public service media (PSM) face a democratic dilemma when covering right-wing populism: They are expected to be impartial and ensure pluralism in their reporting. This requires them to give visibility to a range of views, including those of far-right parties, while safeguarding democratic principles such as non-discrimination and human dignity. In this context, it is essential to understand how PSM representatives reflect on their reporting of right-wing populism. Drawing on the concept of journalistic roles, this study examines PSM representatives’ narrated role performance and role orientation in reporting on populism. Based on semi-structured interviews with twenty-six German PSM representatives, the findings reveal that journalists perceive past reporting mistakes and partly believe that PSM have contributed to the rise of populism. Regarding their role orientation, PSM representatives emphasize that they do not wish to exclude populist actors from coverage unless they make anti-constitutional statements, a stance they justify by the democratic election of these figures. More broadly, PSM representatives take on three distinct role orientations when reporting on populism: as disseminators, they aim to provide neutral coverage of events; as watchdogs, they seek to critically scrutinize populist actors; and as educators, they strive to inform the public about problematic developments within populist parties. Against the backdrop of the growing support for the German populist party Alternative für Deutschland (AfD), this study contributes to existing research by proposing a conceptual framework for reporting on populism and offering qualitative insights into how PSM representatives navigate their role in covering the AfD.
abstract_zh: 公共服务媒体在报道右翼民粹主义时面临民主困境：他们被期望保持公正并确保报道的多元性。这要求他们呈现包括极右翼政党在内的各种观点，同时维护非歧视和人类尊严等民主原则。在此背景下，理解公共服务媒体代表如何反思其对右翼民粹主义的报道至关重要。本研究借鉴新闻角色概念，考察公共服务媒体代表在报道民粹主义时所叙述的角色表现和角色取向。基于对26位德国公共服务媒体代表的半结构化访谈，研究发现记者认识到过去的报道错误，并部分认为公共服务媒体助长了民粹主义的兴起。在角色取向上，公共服务媒体代表强调他们不希望将民粹主义行动者排除在报道之外，除非他们发表违宪言论，他们以这些人物通过民主选举为由证明这一立场。更广泛地说，公共服务媒体代表在报道民粹主义时采取三种不同的角色取向：作为传播者，他们旨在提供中立的事件报道；作为监督者，他们寻求批判性地审视民粹主义行动者；作为教育者，他们努力告知公众民粹主义政党内部的问题发展。在德国民粹主义政党“德国选择党”支持率不断上升的背景下，本研究通过提出报道民粹主义的概念框架，并提供关于公共服务媒体代表如何驾驭报道该党角色的定性见解，为现有研究做出了贡献。
plain_summary_zh: 这项研究探讨了德国公共服务媒体的记者如何报道右翼民粹主义政党（如德国选择党）。记者们承认过去报道有失误，并认为媒体可能助长了民粹主义的兴起。他们表示不会刻意排除民粹主义者的声音，除非其言论违宪。记者在报道中扮演三种角色：中立的信息传播者、批判的监督者和教育公众的引导者。
plain_summary_en: This study explores how journalists at German public service media cover right-wing populist parties like the Alternative for Germany (AfD). Journalists admit past reporting mistakes and believe media may have contributed to the rise of populism. They say they do not exclude populist voices unless they make unconstitutional statements. Journalists take on three roles: neutral disseminators, critical watchdogs, and educators informing the public.
research_direction_zh: 新闻角色与民粹主义报道
